question: How does virtual function dispatch work internally?
What is the difference between late binding and early binding?
options: Late binding resolved at runtime, early at compile time

question: Explain the role of std::is_same in C++ type traits.
options: Checks if two types are identical at compile time

question: What is the concept of type erasure in C++?
options: Hiding concrete type behind abstract interface

question: How does perfect forwarding work with std::forward?
What is a variadic template in C++11?
options: Template that accepts variable number of arguments

question: Which C++ feature enables implementing state machines elegantly?
What is the difference between std::vector and std::array?
options: Vector is dynamic, array is fixed size

question: How does the compiler implement exception handling?
What is the purpose of noexcept specifier in C++11?
options: Indicate function does not throw exceptions

question: What is the difference between std::unique_ptr and std::shared_ptr?
options: unique_ptr has exclusive ownership, shared_ptr has shared ownership

question: How do lambda expressions capture variables by reference?
What is the key advantage of using templates over macros?
Which OOP principle is violated when using friend functions excessively?
Compare Stack and Heap allocation in C++. Which is faster?
options: Stack is faster due to contiguous memory

question: What is encapsulation in OOP?
options: Binding data and methods together, hiding internal state

question: What is the difference between a class and an object?
options: Class is a blueprint; object is an instance
Object is a blueprint; class is an instance
Class is runtime, object is compile-time

question: Which access specifier allows access within the same class only?
What is inheritance in C++?
options: Deriving a new class from an existing class

question: Which type of inheritance leads to the diamond problem?
How is the diamond problem resolved in C++?
What is polymorphism in OOP?
options: Ability to take many forms, same interface different implementations

question: Which of the following is an example of compile-time polymorphism?
What is function overloading in C++?
options: Multiple functions with same name but different parameters
Function with same name and parameters
Defining a function inside a class

question: What is operator overloading?
options: Using operators only for built-in types

question: What is a constructor in C++?
options: Special member function called when object is created

question: What is a destructor in C++?
options: Special member function called when object is destroyed

question: What is a copy constructor used for?
options: Initialize an object from another object of the same type

question: What is a virtual destructor?
options: Ensures proper cleanup of derived objects through base pointer

question: What is a pure virtual function?
options: Virtual function with no body, makes class abstract

question: What is an abstract class?
options: Class with at least one pure virtual function

question: What is the 'this' pointer in C++?
options: Pointer to the current object instance

question: What is a friend function in C++?
options: Non-member function with access to private members
Member function of the same class

question: What is a static data member in C++?
options: Shared across all objects of the class

question: What is a static member function?
options: Can access only static members, no this pointer

question: What is a namespace in C++?
options: Scope container to avoid name collisions

question: What is the Standard Template Library (STL)?
options: Collection of containers, algorithms, and iterators

question: Which STL container stores key-value pairs with unique keys?
What is an iterator in C++ STL?
options: Object that points to elements in a container

question: What is the difference between a struct and a class in C++?
options: Struct has default public; class has default private

question: What is a const member function?
options: Member function that does not modify object state

question: What is a mutable keyword used for?
options: Allow modification of a member in a const function

question: What is a reference variable in C++?
What is the difference between a pointer and a reference?
options: Pointer can be reassigned; reference cannot

question: What is dynamic memory allocation in C++?
options: Allocating memory at runtime using new/delete

question: What happens if a virtual function is called from a constructor?
options: Base class version is called, not derived

question: What is the Rule of Three in C++?
options: If you need destructor, copy ctor, or copy assignment, define all three

question: What is move semantics in C++11?
options: Transfer resources from temporary objects efficiently

question: What does the auto keyword do in C++11?
options: Deduces type from initializer at compile time

question: What is a lambda expression in C++11?
options: Anonymous function object that can capture variables
